LAW AND MOTION,PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT BAUER INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF JIMMY ENGELMAN.(PER EX PARTE ORDER FILED 1/25/19)BAUER'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION IS GRANTED. THE DEPOSITION SHALL OCCUR BY FEBRUARY 28, 2019. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE TIME AND PLACE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT ENCOURAGES THE PARTIES TO STIPULATE TO A TRIAL CONTINUANCE AND OBTAIN AN ORDER FROM DEPARTMENT 206. IF SUCH A CONTINUANCE IS GRANTED, THE DEPOSITION MAY OCCUR AT THE LATEST 14 CALENDAR DAYS PRIOR TO THE NEW TRIAL DATE. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) STIPULATION AND ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Bauer Intelligent Transportation, Inc.'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Jimmy Engelman.(Per Ex Parte Order Filed 1/25/19)
Off Calendar Feb-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Beach, Esq. 510-596-0888 of Bishop Barry for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with a modification as to the Deposition date as follows:

Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Bauer Intelligent Transportation, Inc.'s Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Jimmy Engelman.(Per Ex Parte Order Filed 1/25/19)

Bauer's motion to compel deposition is GRANTED. The deposition shall occur by February 28, 2019. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the time and place. In addition, the court encourages the parties to stipulate to a trial continuance and obtain an order from Department 206. If such a continuance is granted, the deposition may occur at the latest 14 calendar days prior to the new trial date. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Stipulation and Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

PLAINTIFF BAUER INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s Motion For Preliminary Injunctive Relief is continued to April 10, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, per the parties' stipulation. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
